api/client/javascript/src.purpose: Public TypeScript SDK surface for OpenMeter — organizes the generated admin client, portal-scoped client, React integration, and Zod validation schemas as four independent sub-packages. No source files live here directly; it is a routing layer whose children split by concern (admin API, portal API, framework binding, runtime validation).

patterns.name: Generated-first, never hand-edited
patterns.description: src/client/schemas.ts and src/zod/index.ts are fully generated from the OpenAPI spec via openapi-fetch and orval. Any manual edit is overwritten by `make gen-api`.
patterns.example: // schemas.ts and zod/index.ts headers contain 'generated' markers — treat as read-only

patterns.name: Separate admin vs portal client
patterns.description: src/client/ is the full admin SDK; src/portal/ is a restricted portal-token-only client. These must never be merged: portal client intentionally omits all admin endpoints.
patterns.example: // portal/index.ts: class OpenMeter { constructor({ portalToken }: Config) }

patterns.name: React integration via context/provider/hook trio
patterns.description: src/react/ exposes exactly one context, one provider, and one hook. All SDK logic delegates to src/portal/; react/ only handles injection into the component tree.

patterns.name: transformResponse wraps all fetch calls
patterns.description: Every public method in src/client/ must pipe the raw fetch result through transformResponse (handles date decoding and error mapping). Portal client reuses the same utility from src/client/utils.js.

patterns.name: RequestOptions threaded through every public method
patterns.description: Every public SDK method accepts options?: RequestOptions and spreads ...options into the fetch call to propagate abort signals.

key_file_guides.role: Root OpenMeter admin class — instantiates all resource classes and exposes them as public fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New resource classes must be registered here as a field; forgetting this makes the resource unreachable from the public API.

key_file_guides.role: Generated TypeScript types for all OpenAPI paths/operations — source of truth for request/response shapes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never edit manually; all domain types in client/ must be imported from here, not redefined locally.

key_file_guides.role: Shared utilities: transformResponse, encodeDates, date serialization for query params.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Portal client imports these too — any signature change here breaks both clients.

key_file_guides.role: Restricted portal client — authenticates via portalToken, exposes only the portal meter query endpoint.
key_file_guides.watch_for: portalToken must never be optional; do not add admin endpoints here.

key_file_guides.role: React context/provider/hook for injecting portal OpenMeter client. Must keep 'use client' directive for Next.js App Router compatibility.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Context default is undefined (not null); useOpenMeter throws on undefined but treats null as 'not logged in'. Do not change this distinction.

key_file_guides.role: Generated Zod schemas for all API shapes — runtime type-safety layer over the TypeScript types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit; do not import in Go server code — TypeScript only.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing schemas.ts or zod/index.ts — both are generated and will be overwritten by `make gen-api`
Adding admin-level endpoints to src/portal/ — the portal client is intentionally restricted to portal-scoped access only
Redefining transformResponse or encodeDates inside portal/ or react/ instead of importing from client/utils.js
Adding a new resource class to src/client/ without registering it as a public field on the root OpenMeter class in index.ts
Removing the 'use client' directive from react/context.tsx — breaks Next.js App Router server components

decisions.decision: Four sub-packages (client, portal, react, zod) instead of a single entry point
decisions.rationale: Separates concerns: generated types/schemas from hand-authored utilities, admin from portal access, framework-agnostic client from React-specific integration.

decisions.decision: Portal client is a separate class, not a configuration mode of the admin client
decisions.rationale: Enforces at the type level that portal consumers cannot call admin endpoints; avoids accidental capability escalation.

decisions.decision: Zod schemas are generated by orval from the OpenAPI spec rather than hand-authored
decisions.rationale: Keeps runtime validation in sync with the TypeSpec-derived spec without manual maintenance; any spec change propagates via `make gen-api`.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new resource class to the admin SDK client
